items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** PR #57773 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57773 introduced the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (commit `f36c2590fdd199291297b197fc5b56a1040cb2e7`), providing declarative provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers. The package includes 10+ resource types: `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`, `AdvancedThreatProtection`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — the Getting Started section has a heading but no content explaining how to authenticate - **"Key concepts" section is skeletal** — only one generic sentence; none of the key resource types (`MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, etc.) are listed or explained - **"Examples" section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to provision a MySQL flexible server, create a database, or configure a firewall rule - **"Next steps" section is completely empty** — no links to Azure documentation, related packages, or samples - **No sample test files exist** — `tests/Samples/` is empty so there are no snippet-backed examples to auto-generate
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` is a provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates for deployment via `azd` or the Azure CLI. No runtime credentials are required to define infrastructure. Authentication is only needed when deploying the generated templates, which is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the skeletal `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` allows you to define Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server infrastructure declaratively in .NET. The library generates Bicep templates that can be deployed using `azd` or the Azure CLI. Key resource types available in this library: | Resource Type | Description | |---|---| | `MySqlFlexibleServer` | The primary MySQL Flexible Server resource | | `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | A database hosted on the flexible server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | IP firewall rules for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | Server parameter configurations | | `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | Azure Active Directory administrator for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` | Backup resources for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | Scheduled maintenance windows | | `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | Private endpoint connections | | `AdvancedThreatProtection` | Advanced threat protection settings | For a full conceptual overview of the provisioning library pattern, see the [Azure.Provisioning README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md). ``` Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ```C# Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer ``` ``` (The actual code content will be injected by `Update-Snippets.ps1` once the sample test file is created in Step 2.) Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI (azd) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning library README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [NuGet package]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples; public class Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [IgnoreOnCoverage] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer // Create a new infrastructure definition var infrastructure = new Infrastructure("mysqlDemo"); // Define a MySQL Flexible Server var server = new MySqlFlexibleServer("mysqlserver") { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = "adminUser", AdministratorLoginPassword = new BicepSecretValue("adminPassword"), Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled } }; infrastructure.Add(server); // Add a database to the server var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase("mydb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infrastructure.Add(database); // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infrastructure.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } ``` **README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`. **SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer` ### Step 3: Verify README structure All required sections exist in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` but the following are missing content: 1. ✅ Getting started / Install the package — present 2. ✅ Getting started / Prerequisites — present 3. ❌ Getting started / Authenticate the client — **empty, needs content** (see Step 1) 4. ❌ Key concepts — **skeleton only, needs expansion** (see Step 1) 5. ❌ Examples — **empty, needs snippet placeholder** (see Steps 1 and 2) 6. ✅ Troubleshooting — present 7. ❌ Next steps — **empty, needs links** (see Step 1) 8. ✅ Contributing — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code from the test file into README.md placeholders; verify README was updated with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — regenerates API listing files if any public types were changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [MySql] Docs: Complete README for new Azure.Provisioning.MySql package
